channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: How HIV Affects Older Adults Differently
Propofol is associated with lower mortality in sepsis-associated encephalopathy
item.description: 1. Among patients with sepsis-associated encephalopathy, propofol monotherapy was associated with lower one-year all-cause mortality compared to no se...

item.title: Reasons Why Women Experience Hair Thinning
Exercise Benefits for a Faster Metabolism
item.description: More intense workouts can temporarily raise your metabolic rate. Learn how exercise affects metabolism and which workouts have the greatest impact.

item.title: FDA Approves Wearable Device to Treat Pancreatic Cancer
item.description: The manufacturer says the approval is the first for locally advanced pancreatic cancer in 30 years. Medscape Medical News

item.title: Specialized microscopy enables researchers to visualize the dynamics of myelin swellings
item.description: An international research team has gained new insights into the dynamics of myelin swellings in the brain. Myelin swellings are considered as the prec...

item.title: Infection prevention measures prove important in NICU, study shows
item.description: A new study conducted by clinician-scientists at a dozen neonatal intensive care units, or NICUs, across North America found that enhanced infection p...

item.title: Business literacy empowers physicians to lead sustainable health systems [PODCAST]

item.title: Study finds distinct gut bacteria in newly diagnosed children with Crohn's disease
item.description: NYU researchers have found a microbial signature of pediatric Crohn's disease that differs from the makeup of gut bacteria in children with other gast...

item.title: Immigrant caregiver burden: the hidden cost of the five-year Medicaid wait

item.title: Mindfulness Boosts Tolerance of Awake Endoscopy
item.description: Teaching breathing and relaxation techniques before awake endoscopy improved tolerance and supported early cancer detection in outpatients. Medscape N...

item.title: Nearly 38% of Cancers Potentially Avoidable: Global Study
item.description: Three major modifiable risk factors account for much of the attributable cancer burden, but substantial variations across regions and between sexes ex...

item.title: 'Astounding' Number of Americans Microdosing Psychedelics
item.description: About 10 million US adults microdosed psilocybin, LSD, or MDMA in 2025, a new probability-based, nationally representative survey shows. Medscape Medi...

item.title: Nonablative Radiofrequency Improves Menopause Sexual Health
item.description: Nonablative capacitive-resistive monopolar radiofrequency therapy improved sexual function and vaginal health in postmenopausal women with genitourina...

item.title: Pregnant women in Brazil unaware of their rights suffer violations in the workplace

item.title: Connected health care workflows: From chore to core patient care
item.description: Today’s clinicians are spending more time documenting care than delivering it. This reality is not lost on health care providers, many of whom feel bu...

item.title: Mitochondrial RNA may contribute to improved wound healing in diabetes
item.description: Diabetic foot ulcers are slow-healing and increase the risk of infections and amputation. Researchers at Karolinska Institutet have identified a circu...

item.title: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine®: Daily caffeine intake linked to lower dementia risk, AAP sues over federal vaccine schedule overhaul, Catherine O’Hara passes away after short illness, and GLP-1 receptor agonists reduce endometrial cancer risk
item.description: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine® is a pop-culture medical newsletter and exclusive benefit for 2 Minute Medicine Plus subscribers. Moderate caffeine int...

item.title: Polypharmacy is associated with higher risk of mortality and hospitalizations among older adults
item.description: 1. Polypharmacy was associated with a higher risk of mortality and hospitalization at one-year and five-years among older adults aged >75 years. Evide...

item.title: The necessity of getting lost to find yourself
item.description: We are born into corridors, measured, labeled, painted neutral, walls rising before we learn how wide our thoughts can stretch. Lines are drawn early:...

item.title: Physician resilience: Why systems matter more than heroism
item.description: Medicine has always loved the story of the hero. The physician who pushes through exhaustion. The one who catches the rare diagnosis at the last secon...

item.title: Status epilepticus cause may predict likelihood of neurologic recovery but not short-term mortality
item.description: 1. The underlying cause of status epilepticus is strongly associated with the likelihood of neurologic recovery, but does not independently predict sh...

item.title: Oral nonstatin medication significantly lowers cholesterol in high-risk patients
